pattern: Dynamic programming: find the maximum digit count reachable at each cost budget, then greedily reconstruct the largest number by choosing the biggest digit at each step.
hints: First compute dp[j] = maximum number of digits you can paint using exactly cost j. Use -infinity to mark unreachable budgets.
A larger number always has more digits than a shorter one, so maximising digit count is the primary objective.
To reconstruct: for each position greedily try digit 9 down to 1 — pick the first digit d where dp[remaining - cost[d-1]] == dp[remaining] - 1, then subtract and repeat.

edge_inputs.description: cheapest digit yields the longest number: digit 3 costs 2, target 9 should give 3 threes not nine 9s
exact cost match for a large digit
target equals cost of digit 1 exactly
all costs equal max target, target matches one cost
tie in digit count; greedy picks highest digit

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    cost = [rng.randint(1, 10) for _ in range(9)]
    target = rng.randint(1, min(size, 100))
    return [cost, target]
